mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings bases, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as analysts revise earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness supports consumer spending and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the CPI index indicate aggressive monetary tightening amid persistent inflation, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable during periods of concurrent rate hikes and inflation spikes, or the joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production following a recessionary trough signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns during recovery phases, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 40% of observed recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts exceeding 100,000 units acts as a leading indicator for construction activity and consumer wealth effects, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts surge but equity returns remain flat or decline due to offsetting monetary tightening, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as corporate revenue visibility improves.
failure_condition: Payroll and retail sales growth decouple from equity performance, or the joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Gradual increases in the federal funds rate raise the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand or remain stable during sustained rate hiking cycles, or the signal predicts negative returns in less than 40% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth stalls while the CPI index remains elevated signals stagflationary pressure, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn pessimistic.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite stagnant growth and sticky inflation, or the divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.
